key_insight: IL-34 loss-of-function primes microglia toward Alzheimer’s disease-associated, inflammatory states and alters CSF proteomic networks linked to neurodegeneration.
clinical_relevance: The IL-34-Y213X variant may help identify people at higher AD risk and points to IL-34–microglia signaling as a potential biomarker or therapeutic axis.
method: Human genetics plus CSF/serum proteomics in AD cohorts
Microglial transcriptomics in IL-34 knockout mice
APP/PS1 mouse and postmortem human brain analyses
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a dose-dependent IL-34-Y213X effect with up to 2.5 SD lower IL-34 in CSF and serum, alongside converging human, omics, and animal-model evidence.
watch_item: Findings are associative and preclinical, so translation to diagnosis or treatment will require independent replication and functional validation.
paper_title: Human IL-34 Deficiency Primes Microglia Toward Alzheimer's Disease-Associated States
